def: v.n. To be inclined; to incline, slope (towards); to roll, move or slip forward, to yield, slip, sink (cf. ḍhalnā); — to be poured out, be spilt ( = ḍhalnā).
v.n. To be washed
subs: v.n. To be washed, to be washed out or away (from, -se).

def: v.n. To be carried, conveyed, transported, or removed.
s.f. = dhulāʼī, q.v.
s.f. = ḍhulāʼī, q.v.
v.t. = dhulānā, q.v.
v.t. = ḍhulānā, q.v.

ety: S. ध्वनिः; or fr. ध्मा; cf. धम
def: s.f. Noise, bustle, bluster, tumult, uproar; report, rumour, fame; celebrity, display, parade, pomp, ado
subs: To make a noise, create an uproar; to make a stir or ado; to cry aloud, to roar
dhūm honā, v.n. To be noised abroad, to resound, to be or become famous or notorious.

def: s.m. = dhūm-dhām.
s.m. Smoke-colour, a mixture of red and black, a purple, a dun colour.
s.m. Smoke; vapour; mist, haze
subs: s.f. Name of one of the seventeen divisions of hell, where smoke takes the place of light
adj. & s.m. 'Having smoke as a sign or flag'; known or to be known by smoke; — fire; a comet; a falling star
adj. Consisting of smoke or vapour, abounding in smoke, &c., smoky.

def: adj. = dhumlā, q.v.
adj. Noisy, tumultuous, uproarious, boisterous; quarrelsome, turbulent.
adj. (f. -ī), Smoky; hazy, misty, foggy; dim, dull, gloomy, dark; dim-sighted, blind.

ety: dhūm+Prk. अलओ or इल्लओ = S. अल or इल+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī) = dhūmrā, q.v.

ety: dhūm, 'noise,' q.v.+Prk. अडओ = S. अ+र or ल +कः
def: s.m. Tumult, bustle, uproar, confusion ( = dhūm).

ety: dhumlā+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. Haziness, mistiness; dimness, dulness; gloominess, darkness ( = dhundhlāī).
adj. Bulky, fat, corpulent.
adj. = dhumlā, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī) Smoke-coloured, grey, blue, of the colour of black and red mixed, purple, dun; dark, lurid.

ety: dhūm, 'noise,' q.v.+Prk. अडओ = S. अ+र or ल +कः
def: s.m. Tumult, bustle, uproar, confusion ( = dhūm).

ety: Prk. धुणी; S. धुनिः, rt. धु, or धूनिः, rt. धू
def: s.f. Agitation; longing, ardent desire, ardour, passion; ambition; inclination, propensity; diligence, assiduity, application, perseverance; — racking pain in the bones ( = dhunihāʼo; cf. dhunnā).
s.f. = دهني ḍhuni, q.v.
s.f. Explosion, report (of a gun); sound (of a drum).
s.m. Gum, rosin, resin (esp. that of the Shorea robusta).
s.m. Resin (esp. that of the Shorea robusta; see dhūnā).

ety: dhunak˚ = Prk. धुणक्क(इ), or धुणक्के(इ), fr. S. ध्वनि+कृ
def: v.n. To sound, resound, reverberate, boom (as cannon, &c.).

ety: dhunak˚ = Prk. धुणक्क(इ) or धुणक्के(इ), fr. S. धूनि (rt. धू)+कृ
def: v.t. To card or comb (cotton), to separate (cotton).

ety: Ap. Prk. धूवँउ, Prk. धूमओ = S. धूम+कः
def: s.m. Smoke, vapour (dhūʼāṅ, dhūṅwā).
s.m. = dhūṅwālā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = dhundh, q.v.
s.m. = ḍhūṅḍh, q.v.
s.m. = ḍhūṅḍh or ḍhoṅḍh, q.v.
s.m. Design, intention, attempt; idea, thought.

s.m. Haziness, mistiness, fogginess, haze, mist, fog; cloudiness, darkness, gloom; thick cloudy weather; a dust-cloud; dim-sightedness, purblindness; — adj. Hazy, misty, &c. ( = dhundhlā); bereft of sense or consciousness, intoxicated, besotted, stupefied
subs: The region of darkness, the world
v.t. To make foggy, &c.; to intoxicate, to stupefy.

ety: prob. S. ध्वस्तः, or by redupl. fr. ध्वंस्
def: s.m. A dilapidated house, a ruin.
s.m. Seeking, search, quest, close or strict search, inquiry, investigation
subs: To search, make a close or strict search, &c. ( = ḍhūṅḍhnā-ḍhāṅḍhnā.

def: s.m. Dawn of day, early morning, twilight

def: s.m. Seeking. search ( = ḍhūṅḍh).
s.f. = dhundh, q.v.
s.f. = dhundhar, q.v.
s.m. Making search, search, searching, &c. ( = ḍhūṅḍh, and ḍhūṅḍh-ḍhāṅḍh, qq.v.).
s.m. = ḍhanḍhorā, q.v.
s.m. A devotee or mendicant of the Jain sect.
adj. Producing or causing haziness or mistiness, beclouding, mistifying.

ety: dhundh, q.v.+Prk. अल्लओ or अलओ = S. अ+ल+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Hazy, misty, foggy, cloudy; dull, dim (as sight, &c.).

ety: dhundhlā+āhaṭ = āwaṭ = Prk. ववट्टी = S. वृत्तिः; — and āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+ इका
def: s.f. = dhundhlāpan, q.v.

ety: dhundhlā+āhaṭ = āwaṭ = Prk. ववट्टी = S. वृत्तिः; — and āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+ इका
def: s.f. = dhundhlāpan, q.v.
v.n. To be or become misty, or foggy, &c.; to become dull or dim.

ety: dhundhlā+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Haziness, mistiness, fogginess, cloudiness, obscurity, dulness, dimness ( = dhumlāʼī, q.v.).
s.m. = dhundhlāpan, q.v.
v.t. = ḍhūṅḍhnā, q.v.; — v.n. To be searched.

ety: ḍhūṅḍh˚ = Prk. ढुंढ(इ) = S. ढुण्ढ(ति), rt. ढुण्ढ
def: v.t. To seek, search for
subs: v.t. To seek, search for; to make close or strict search for, make strict inquiry or investigation into
(-ko), To seek and bring, seek out
v.t. To seek out, find out, ferret out
s.m. Seeker, searcher, one by whom an inquiry is carried on ( = ḍhūṅḍhne-wālā).

def: adj. = dhundhlā, q.v.
adj. = dhundhrā, dhundhlā, q.v.
v.n. = dhundhlānā, q.v.

s.m. Name of an Asur or demon (slain by Kuvalayāśva, father of Sunda): dhundhu-mār, s.m. 'Slayer of Dhundhu,' an epithet of Kuvalayāśva; — a species of insect, coccinella (syn. indra-gop, and bīr-bahuṭṭī).

s.m. Heavy rain obsouring the whole heaven; gloomy or cloudy weather, clouds; gloominess, cloudiness, obscurity; desolateness; vaporization; evaporation; waste; — name of a game; — adv Heavily (raining, so as to obscure the sky).
s.m. See next.

s.m. Heavy rain obsouring the whole heaven; gloomy or cloudy weather, clouds; gloominess, cloudiness, obscurity; desolateness; vaporization; evaporation; waste; — name of a game; — adv Heavily (raining, so as to obscure the sky).

s.m. Heavy rain obsouring the whole heaven; gloomy or cloudy weather, clouds; gloominess, cloudiness, obscurity; desolateness; vaporization; evaporation; waste; — name of a game; — adv Heavily (raining, so as to obscure the sky).

ety: Prk. dhundhu (see dhundh)+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आनि (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणतअणअं = S. अनीय
def: v.n. To be filled with smoke, to abound with smoke.
v.t. = ḍhuṅḍhwānā, q.v.
v.t. To cause (one) to seek; to have or get searched.

s.f. Seed-vessel, pod, &c. (esp. of gram, or pulse); — ḍhoṅḍī, s.f. A kind of grass (that grows in rice fields, and sometimes chokes the crop; it is used for thatching and bedding); — a kind of reed (of which qalams or pens are made).
s.m. = ḍhūṅḍhiyā, q.v.
adj. = dhundhlā, q.v.
v.n. = ḍhunḍhnā.
v.t. = ḍhūṅḍhnā, q.v.
s.m. = dhuniyā, q.v.
s.f. The wife of a dhuniyā or cotton-carder; a woman who cards or cleans cotton.
s.m. Seasoning with which anything is stirred and fried (syn. baghār; ćhauṅk).
v.t. To season with spices.
s.f. Sound, inusical sound, air, tune; key-note; — a frog.
s.f. Persevering, &c. ( = dhūnī, q.v.).

s.f. Smoke; the smoke-fire over which a Hindū ascetic sits inhaling the smoke by way of penance (it is also often practised in the manner of dharnā, q.v., to extort compliance with demands); fumigation, or the burning of incense (to purify the air, or as a medical application, or to exorcise one possessed)
subs: s.m. Familiar friendship, intimacy, attachment (from the assumption that, in the former birth, one of the parties was a guru, and the other his disciple, employed in kindling for him the dhūnī, bringing his pānī, &c.)
(-ko), To smoke, to fumigate (either medicinally, or in order to exorcise one possessed); — to dun, to importune (see dhūnī lagānā)
dhūnī lagānā, v.n. To light a fire and sit over it after the manner of jogīs; to become a jogī
v.n. To have recourse to the smoking process in order to extort compliance with a demand, to persevere, or to insist obstinately, in a demand; — to fumigate, &c. ( = dhūnī denā)
(-kī), To inhale smoke (as a penance); to undergo fumigation.

def: s.f. A river.
adj. Persevering, assiduous, using strenuous effort.

ety: prob. fr. a form dhuniyā(nā) = dhun(nā); — āʼo = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं
def: s.m. Racking pains in the bones ( = dhun).
s.m. = dhunihāʼo, q.v.
s.m. A carder or cleaner (of cotton).
s.m. Chimney (of a sugar-mill, &c.; cf. dhūṅrā, dhūṅʼārā, dhūṅwālā).
s.f. Noise report, explosion; roar, bellowing, lowing.
v.n. To make a noise, to roar, bellow, thunder.
v.t. = دهننا dhunnā, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) = dhuniyā, q.v.

ety: dhun˚ = Prk. धुण(इ) = S. धूनी(ति), rt. धू

s.m. = dhūṅʼārā, dhūṅwālā, q.v.
v.t. (rustic) = ḍhūṅḍhnā, q.v.
v.t. (rustic) = ḍhūṅḍhnā, q.v.
s.m. = ḍhūsar, q.v.
v.t. = dhūsnā, q.v.

s.m. Smoke, &c. ( = dhūʼāṅ, dhūṅwā, qq.v.).

s.m. = dhūṅwālā, q.v.
v.t. To smoke.

s.m. Incense, frankincense; any fragrant gum or resin (burnt before idols); vapour proceeding from fragrant gum or resin, aromatic vapour or smoke, perfume; — (also, in Hindī and Urdū) s.f. Sunlight, sunshine, heat of the sun, the sun; — prickly heat
subs: v.n. To be sunny, to be hot
dhūp ćaṛhānā, To burn incense (before an idol, &c.)
v.n. The sun to rise, or to be high
s.f. Sunshine and shade; — shot silk
s.m., or dhūp-dānī, s.f. Incensepot, censer; box for keeping incense
s.f. The burning of incense (before a Jain idol) on the tenth of the light fortnight of Bhādoṅ
v.t. To put in the sun (to dry, &c.), to sun
s.m. Offering of incense (to an idol)
s.m. Hot weather, the hot season
v.n. To bask in the sun
(or bāl) safaid karnā, 'To whiten one's hair in the sun'; to become gray-headed without gaining experience or wisdom; to pass life in ignorance
vulg. dhūp-br̤iksh, s.m. 'Incense-tree'; a species of pine, Pinus longifolia
s.f. A clouded sun, a very hot sun.

ety: plur. of dhupelī = dhūp+elī = Prk. इल्लिआ = S. इल+इका; cf. ghamaurī
def: s.f. Prickly heat.

ety: dhūp˚ = S. धूपय(ति), fr. धूप;+nā = Prk. अण्मअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To burn incense to or before; to fumigate; to perfume; to besmear with resin or pitch.
s.m. Beginning; limit, end, extremity; — adj. Extreme, remote, far distant
subs: s.m. A kind of song (in the Hindī or Braj), the introductory stanza of a poem or song, which is repeated as a kind of burden or chorus
adv. From beginning to end, throughout, wholly
v.n. To be doomed from the first, the thread of life to be spun out.

def: s.m. A yoke; a load; the fore part of a pole where the yoke is fixed; the head (as the place of honour)
subs: or dhuran-dhar, adj. & s.m. Bearing the yoke, bearing a burden; helping; strong in supporting, mighty; — a beast of burden; a man of business; a chief, leader; — name of a people; name of a Rākshas.

def: s.m. = ḍhur, q.v.
s.f. Dust ( = dhūl, q.v.)
subs: s.m. One who is covered with dust; a dirty or shabby person.

def: s.f. A coarse kind of grass; — the twentieth part of a biswā.
s.m. The hip; a cross-buttock(?)
subs: or ḍhuṛ-par uṛānā, v.t. To throw (one) across the hip, to give (one) a cross-buttock
v.t. To raise or place (one) on the hip (in wrestling).

def: s.m. An axle, axle-tree; the wooden portion of an axle; a pole, yoke; an axis.

ety: S. ध्रुवकः, rt. ध्रु; see dhur
def: s.m. Border or limits of a village or field, &c.), a boundary, a landmark.
s.m. An axle, &c. ( = dhurā, q.v.).
s.m. Trick, deception, &c. ( = dhuttā; dhokhā)
subs: (-ko), To deceive, trick, wheedle, take in.

def: adj. See s.v. dhur, 'a yoke.'
s.m. A tall stout fellow; a bulky or unwieldy person, a monster.
s.f. A firelock (without a chamber).
adj. & s.m. See s.v. dhur, 'yoke.'
s.f. = dhuleṅḍī, q.v.
v.t. (dialec.) = ḍhūṅḍhnā, q.v.

ety: Prk. धुरिआ = S. धुर+इका; see dhurā
def: s.f. Pole; iron part of an axle, axle-tree, axle; axis.
s.f. Dust ( = dhūli, q.v.).
s.f. 1˚ = dhuri, q.v.; — 2˚ Strand of a rope (cf. dohrā).
s.m. Beast of burden; — man of business; head-man, leader, chief.
s.m. A species of large jasmine.
s.m. Name of a musical mode (sung particularly at the beginning of the rains).

ety: S. धूलि+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि caus. augment
def: v.t. To throw dust upon; — to winnow, sift, separate good grain from bad.
v.t. 'To blow away dust, &c. from'; to winnow grain a second time.

ety: prob. dhur, q.v.+kaṭ, rt. of kāṭnā
def: s.f. (?), Advance of rent paid by villagers to the landlord in the months of Jeṭh and Asārh.
s.f. The twentieth part of a dhūr, or 1⁄400 of a biswā.
v.t. (dialec.) = ḍhūṅḍhnā, q.v.
s.m. See s.v. dhur.
s.m. A strip of cloth ( = dhajjī)
subs: dhurre uṛānā or uṛā-denā = dhajjiyāṅ uṛānā

def: s.m. Evening, dusk ( = dhur-sāṅjh, q.v.s.v. dhur).
adj. & s.m. Cunning, crafty, sly, dishonest, fraudulent, knavish; mischievous, injurious; — a cheat, knave, rogue; a gamester.
s.f. = S دهورتتو धूर्तत्व dhūrtatva, s.m. Shrewdness, craftiness, slyness, knavishness, knavery, fraudulence, roguery; cheating.
s.m. = S دهورتتا धूर्तता dhūrtatā, s.f. Shrewdness, craftiness, slyness, knavishness, knavery, fraudulence, roguery; cheating.
s.m. A pea (cf. ḍhonḍ).
s.f. Cramming and stuffing.
adj. & s.m. Of a dusty-white colour, dust-coloured, grey; — the colour grey.
s.m. Name of a Hindū caste (they are really Vaiśyas, but call themselves Brāhmans); an individual of this caste.

ety: dhūs˚, prob. = ध्वंसय(ति), caus. of rt. ध्वंस्; but cf. ṭhūsnā, ṭhosnā
def: v.t. To ram, stuff, cram down; — to butt, to gore (as horned cattle).

ety: S. दूर्श+कं or धूष्य+कं; cf. dussā
def: s.m. A coarse kind of woollen stuff; flannel; blanket.
s.f. The thorn-apple, Datura fastuosa ( = dhatūrā, q.v.).
adj. Steeped (in, -meṅ), stupefied (by, liquor, &c.), besotted (with).
intj. Away! be off! ( = dut, q.v.)
subs: intj. Away with you! be off! — dhat-dhat, intj. Idem
dhut-kārnā, v.t. To reprove, revile; to drive away.

def: s.f. A wife.
s.m. Good behaviour.
v.t. To cheat, trick, defraud, to deceive, play (one) false.
s.m. Trick, trickery, knavery, deception
s.f. The introductory stanza of a song (forming afterwards the burden of each verse; cf. dhur-pad).
v.t. = dhulānā, q.v.
adj. = dhūṅwar, q.v.
s.m. Flag, banner, standard, &c. ( = dhajā, q.v.); — a mark, sign, symbol; — the organ of generation, the penis ( = dhaj).
s.m. Flag, banner, standard, &c. ( = dhajā, q.v.); — a mark, sign, symbol; — the organ of generation, the penis ( = dhaj).
adj. & s.m. Furnished with, or bearing a flag; — a standard-bearer.
s.m. Sound, tune
subs: s.m. lit. 'Delighting by its sound,' the humble-bee.

def: s.f. = دهوني dhvani, q.v.
s.f. Sound, noise; echo; tone, tune; the sound of a drum
subs: s.f. A sort of trumpet; the bīn or lute; a pipe, fife, &c.

def: part. Sounded; giving forth a noise (as a drum); alluded to, figuratively or rhetorically expressed.
s.m. A crow; — a crane, gull, any aquatic bird feeding on fish.
s.m. Falling down, destruction, demolition; loss, ruin
adj. & s.m. Destroying, destructive; — destruction, ruin, loss.
s.f. A mote in a sunbeam.
adj. Falling, perishing, being destroyed, decaying; destroying, destructive.
part. Thrown down, demolished, destroyed, ruined, lost.
corr. of dhvajā, q.v.
s.m. A kind of fragrant grass; the plant Artemisia indica (syn. daun).

s.m. Meditation, contemplation (especially profound and abstract religious contemplation); divine intuition or discernment; consideration, imagination (esp. that profound and abstract consideration which brings objects fully and undisturbedly before the mind); mental representation of the personal attributes of the divinity to whom worship may happen to be addressed; attention, regard, advertency
subs: adj. & s.m. Lost in thought, absorbed in meditation, reflecting, meditative; — one whose highest object is meditation, a person absorbed in meditation
(or -pe) ćaṛhnā, To absorb mental consideration; to take the fancy
(-par), To apply the mind to contemplation, to contemplate, meditate (on); to pay attention or regard (to), to observe, notice
(-par, or -kī or), To pay attention (to), turn or direct the attention (to), to consider, to heed, regard, observe, mind
(-kā, or -par), To think (about), meditate (on), keep the attention fixed (on)
adv. Meditatively; with fixed attention, attentively
(-kā, or -par, or kī or), To contemplate, meditate (on), give thought or attention (to), to think (over), &c. ( = dhyān dharnā and dhyān denā)
(-ke), To be apprehended, be understood, be taken in (mentally)
v.t. To apprehend, understand, take in (mentally); to pay attention or regard to, to heed
vulg. dhiyān-jog, s.m. Devout meditation, the performance of religious abstraction.

def: v.t. (Braj) = dhyānā, q.v.

adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Contemplative, engaged or absorbed in religious meditation, meditating, given to meditation, religious; thoughtful, considerate, attentive; — one who engages in religious meditation, &c.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), To be meditated upon, to be considered; — an object suitable for or requiring meditation or consideration.
v.t. To meditate on, think on, to adore; to know, apprehend.
v.t. = dhyānā, q.v.
v.t. = dhyānā, q.v.
contrac. of diʼī, for diyī, fem. of diyā, perf. part. of denā, q.v.
s.m. = divā, q.v.: — dibā-kar = divā-kar.

r: dībāća, dībāja, dībāj, dībāh, debāh, lit
def: = P ديباجه dībāja (rel. n. fr. dībāj, the arabicized form of P. dībāh or debāh), s.m. lit. 'Having a rich edging of gold, &c.'; frontispiece (of a book); preface, exordium, introduction, preamble.

r: dībāja, dībāća, dībāj, dībāh, debāh, lit
def: = P ديباچه dībāća (rel. n. fr. dībāj, the arabicized form of P. dībāh or debāh), s.m. lit. 'Having a rich edging of gold, &c.'; frontispiece (of a book); preface, exordium, introduction, preamble.
s.m. = divākar, q.v.s.v. divā.
adj. & s.m. Left-handed; — a left-handed person.
s.m. The left hand (cf. debrā).
s.m. A box (generally, a round wooden box, for keeping scents, or jewels, &c. in), a casket (syn. baṭṭā); a cartridge-box.

r: ḍibbī, ḍibī, ḍabbī, ḍabī, ḍibbā, ḍabbā, ḍibiyā
ety: dim. of ḍibbā or ḍabbā; see ḍibiyā
def: s.f. A small box or case, a small casket; (local) a seed; a germ.

ety: dim. of ḍibbā; -iyā = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. A tiny box or casket; — a small portion of grain given at harvest-time to inferior village servants.

ety: dim. of ḍibbā; -iyā = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. A tiny box or casket; — a small portion of grain given at harvest-time to inferior village servants.
adj. & s.m. Pure, unpolluted; — an oath, affidavit, &c. (see divya).
s.f. = dīkshā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = dīkshit, q.v.; — 2˚ Name of a tribe of Kanaujiyā Brāhmans.

ety: Zend dīta; or v.n. of dīdan, 'to see'; Pehl. dītan; rt. Zend dīd or dī = S. dhī (dīdhīte)
def: s.f. (m. rarely), Seeing, sight, vision; show, spectacle
subs: s.f. Looking about one, sight-seeing, taking the air; ogling
s.m. A watch, guard, sentinel, scout, one who keeps a look-out (prob. contrac. of dīda-bān)

def: part. Seen; having seen; — dīd na shunīd, adj. 'Nor seen nor heard of'; wonderful, extraordinary, strange.

ety: Zend dīta; or rel. n. fr. dīd, q.v.
def: s.m. The eye; the sight; a wanton, or impudent eye; (met.) impudence
subs: s.m. Watch, sentinel, overseer, inspector ( = dīd-bān); — the sight (of a gun, &c.)
dīde phāṛ-kar dekhnā, To look steadfastly (at), to stare (at), gaze
adj. f. Having large staring eyes (a woman)
adj. f. Having an eye void of shame, wanton, &c. ( = dīda-dalel)
s.f. A fatiguing or trying the eye (by looking at minute objects, &c.)
To fatigue or try the eye
To throw away all modesty, to become shameless
'To open the eyes,' to consider maturely, regard attentively
(-par), To have the eyes fixed (on), to be intent (on)
To make eyes (at), to wanton with the eyes
To look with angry eyes, to glare (at)
To get into a passion or rage
adv. With heart and soul, cheerfully, willingly
s.f. Sharpness, or clearness, of sight
s.m. An eye void of shame; — adj. = dīda-dhoʼī, q.v.
adj. Saucy-eyed, fearless, bold, impudent, shameless, wanton.

def: part. Seen, observed, perceived, felt, experienced; having seen, &c. (used in comp., e.g. jahān-dīda, jang-dīda, &c.)
subs: adv. 'Having seen and known'; knowingly, wittingly, wilfully, purposely, intentionally.

ety: Pehl. dītan; rt. Zend did or dī = S. धी (दीधी)
def: v.t. To see, look, observe; to perceive, feel, experience; — seeing; experiencing, &c.
adj. To be seen, visible; fit or worthy to be seen.

ety: dī(dan), q.v.+dār = tār = Pehl. tar = Zend tar = S. तार्
def: s.m. Sight, vision ( = dīd); look, appearance; face, countenance, cheek; interview; — overseer, inspector, watch (syn. dīda-dār)
subs: s.m.f., and dīdār-bāzī, s.f. = dīd-bāz, and dīd-bāzī, q.v.s.v. dīd
adj. & s.m. Seeking or soliciting an interview; — one who seeks an interview.

def: adj. Worthy of being seen, well-looking, fair, comely, handsome, personable, presentable.

r: ḍiḍḍhiyā, ḍiḍḍhyā, ḍiḍhiyā, ḍiḍhyā, ḍiḍḍiyā, ḍiḍḍyā, ḍiḍiya, ḍiḍyā
def: s.f. (dialec.) Longing or eager look, covetous eyes; covetousness, greediness
subs: (-par), To fix eager or covetous eyes (on), look covetously (at), to covet, to be greedy, to be insatiable.

r: ḍiḍḍiyā, ḍiḍḍyā, ḍiḍiya, ḍiḍyā, ḍiḍḍhiyā, ḍiḍḍhyā, ḍiḍhiyā, ḍiḍhyā
def: s.f. (dialec.) Longing or eager look, covetous eyes; covetousness, greediness
subs: (-par), To fix eager or covetous eyes (on), look covetously (at), to covet, to be greedy, to be insatiable.

def: adv. corr. of dvi-dhā, q.v.
1˚ (old H.) = diyā, perf. part. of denā; — 2˚ (dialec.) = dvidhā, q.v.
s.f. Fixed state of mind or being, firmness, fixedness, stability.
s.m. The second husband of a woman twice married.
s.f. A virgin widow remarried; an elder sister unmarried having a younger married sister; the childless widow of a brother (whom, under the old law, a surviving brother was required to marry).
s.f. = didishu, q.v.
s.f. Light, brightness, splendour; a ray of light, sun-beam, moon-beam.
s.f. Light, brightness, splendour; a ray of light, sun-beam, moon-beam.
s.m. = dik, q.v.
s.m. See s.v. dik.
s.m. = digambar, q.v.

v.t. To cause to shake or move, to cause to slip, or stumble, or fall, or swerve (from), or relapse, &c.; to shake (as faith, loyalty, &c.), to loosen, to render unsteady or unstable; to displace, dislocate.
adj. & adv. Other, another, next, following ( = dūsrā); — over again, again; moreover, besides, further.
adj. Another, other (pl. digarān); — adv. Once more, again
subs: adj. Of another colour or complexion; altered, changed.

def: v.t. To cause to go away, to send or drive away.
v.n. To chatter (as teeth).
s.f. An oblong tank or pond.
s.f. An oblong tank or pond.

ety: ḍig˚ = ḍag˚ = S. त्रङ्ग(ति), rt. त्रङ्ग, or त्वङ्ग(ति), rt. त्वङ्ग
def: v.n. To shake, tremble, vibrate, quaver; to move, shift, become displaced; to stumble, slip, fall; to swerve (from), deviate, be shaken or moved (as from one's faith, &c.); to shrink, flinch
s.f. A degree; — decree
subs: To issue a decree, to enforce a decree, to take out execution
s.m. Decree-holder, one who takes out a decree, the person in whose favour judgment has been given
ḍigrī karnā, To grant a decree, to decree, adjudge, award.

def: s.f. pl. (of ḍigrī), Degrees; — decrees.
s.m. See s.v. dik.
s.m. See s.v. dik.
s.m. = dih, q.v.
s.f. (old H.) = deh, q.v.

ety: Pārsī dahi; Pehl. dahyū; Zend daṅhu = S. दस्यु
def: s.m. A village
subs: s.f. A detailed statement of villages and their assessment
s.f. The revenue assessment of a village.

ety: fr. dādan, rt. dā = Zend dā = S. दा; — P. dihum = Zend dadhāmi = S. ददामि
def: part. adj. & s.m. Giving, occasioning; — a giver, &c. (used in comp., e.g. ārām-dih, 'Giving ease or comfort, comforting; comforter').

ety: perhaps corr. of dih or dīh; but cf. ḍhī
def: s.m. Haunt, place, dwelling, village; site of a deserted village (cf. ḍhī).
s.m. = dīh or dih, q.v.
s.m. Heap (of earth), mound, bank.
part. & s.m. Giving; willing to pay; — one who gives, or who is willing to give; one who pays, or is able to pay.

ety: dih, q.v.+Zend aff. añṭ = S. अन्त्
def: part. & s.m. Giving; liberal, generous; — a giver, donor; a liberal or beneficent person.

ety: prob. deh, q.v.+āṛā = Prk. वट्टअं = S. वृत्त+कं; or dihā = daśā, q.v.+ṛā = Prk. डओ = S. र+कः
def: s.m. State, condition, plight; wretched condition, miserable plight.

ety: of dih, after the model of the Arabic
def: s.m. Villages held at a fixed rent.
s.m. = P ديہاتي dīhātī, adj. = dihāt, and dihātī, qq.v.
adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to a village, for or relating to the country, rural, rustic; — a villager, a rustic.
adj. = P ديہات dīhāt, s.m. = dihāt, and dihātī, qq.v.

r: dihez, dahez, dahej, jahez, jihāz, jahāz, dejā
ety: fr. P. jahez, fr. A. jihāz or jahāz; but cf. H. dejā
def: s.m. Dowry, portion; a wife's paraphernalia, &c. (see jahez).
adj. = dihī, and dihātī, qq.v.
s.f. Giving, the act of giving (used as last member of compounds).
adj. Of or belonging to a village, village
subs: s.m. A resident cultivator in a village holding the right of cultivation at a fixed rent
s.f. Village custom or practice, village rate.

def: s.f. Liberality, bounty, charity, alms.

r: dihqān, dih, ḵẖān, daqyuma, daqyu, daṅhu, dasyu
ety: dih, 'village'+ḵẖān, qq.v.; A. dihqān; Zend daqyuma, fr. daqyu = daṅhu = dih; S. dasyu
def: s.m. A villager, husbandman, &c. (see dihqānī); head-man of a village.
adj. Rustic, country, boorish, clownish; — s.m. Villager, countryman, rustic, peasant, husbandman; — s.f. The business or profession of a husbandman, agriculture.

r: dihqānīya, dihqāniya, dihqānīyat, dihqāniyat, dihqān, rare
def: s.m. = P دهقانيت dihqānīyat, vulg. dihqāniyat, s.f. (for A. دهقانية fr. dihqān), Husbandry, agriculture (rare); — rusticity, country manners, boorishness, vulgarity.

r: dihqānīyat, dihqāniyat, dihqānīya, dihqāniya, dihqān, rare
def: s.f. = P دهقانيه dihqānīya, vulg. dihqāniya, s.m. (for A. دهقانية fr. dihqān), Husbandry, agriculture (rare); — rusticity, country manners, boorishness, vulgarity.
s.f. Threshold ( = dihuṛī).
prop. n. The city of Dehli, the metropolis of Hindūstān (under the Mohammadan rule; see dillī; and dahal).
s.m. A Hindū temple; a Jain temple ( = dehrā).
s.m. A species of rice plant.
s.f. Threshold, door ( = děhlī, q.v.)
s.f. Threshold, porch, &c. ( = ḍeʼoṛhī, q.v.)
s.f. = ḍeʼoṛhī, q.v.
s.m. The river Tigris (so called because it covers the land with water): — a lake.

ety: fr. दिश्, q.v.; — euphonically changed to dig, in comp., before certain letters; and com. written dig in Hindī
def: s.m. (but fem. in S.), Point of the compass, quarter, direction, side, way; space, region, tract; (in comp.) -wards (e.g. uttar-dig, 'northwards')
subs: adj. & s.m. 'Sky-clothed'; having only the sky or atmosphere for raiment, clad by the regions of space, unclad, stark naked; — a naked person; a naked Hindū ascetic; an order of ascetics of the Jain sect who go naked (if clad in coloured cloth, they are called pītāmbar; if in white, svet-āmbar); a member of that order, a Jain mendicant; an epithet of Śiva (from his being naked), and of certain of his devotees
s.m. The end of space, the horizon, remote distance
s.m. Another region, a distant quarter; aërial space, the atmosphere
s.m. The functions of a dig-pāl or guardian deity of any region

dig-pati, s.m. The regent or guardian of one of the ten regions or points of the compass, or of a quarter of the world (of these guardian deities there are two divisions; 1˚ Astronomical, viz. the Sun, of the east; Saturn, of the west; Mercury, of the North; Mars, of the south; Venus, of the south-east; Rahū, of the south-west; the Moon, of the north-west; and Jupiter, of the north-east; — 2˚ Mythological, viz. Brahma, for upwards; Ananta, for downwards; Indra, of the east; Varuṇa, of the west; Kuvera, of the north; Yama, of the south; Agni, of the south-east; Nirriti, of the south-west; Marut or Vāyu, of the north-west; and Iʼśāna or Śiva, of the north-east)

dig-śūl, s.m. Any inauspicious planetary conjunction (as for the Sun and Venns to be in the west, &c.); see diśā-śūl, s.v. diśā; — dig-gaj, s.m. 'Elephant of the quarter'; a mythical elephant of one of the eight quarters or points (which, with others at different quarters, is supposed to support the world); (met.) great, large, huge
s.m. Point or quarter of the compass, point, quarter, direction
s.m. Subjugation of various countries in all directions; subjugation of extensive tracts of country (whether by arms or controversy), universal conquest
s.m. Invasion of various countries, going forth to conquer the whole world
s.m. Conqueror of many realms, or of the world

def: adj. & s.m. Small, minute, fine; subtle; sagacious, intelligent ( = daqīq, q.v.); — a broken piece or particle, fragment, chip, shred.
s.f. Showing (one self, itself, &c.), coming in sight, appearance; exhibition, show, display
subs: dikhāʼī denā (-ko), To show oneself (or itself, &c.), to come in sight, to appear, be visible or apparent, be seen.

def: v.t. To cause to see, to show; to direct; to denote, indicate; — to exhibit, display, expose, manifest, discover, reveal

ety: dikhāʼo and dikhāwā, i.q. dikhāʼū, q.v.; — dikhāwaṭ = dikhāʼo+Prk. अट्टं = S. क+त्वं
def: Sight, view; exhibition, representation; show, display, ostentation, pomp, pageantry; speciousness, pretence, disguise.
adj. Visible, apparent, to be seen; fit to be shown or exhibited, presentable, comely; for show; showy, ostentatious; specious, ostensible
subs: 'The visible circle which bounds the sight,' the visible horizon.

ety: dikhāʼo and dikhāwā, i.q. dikhāʼū, q.v.; — dikhāwaṭ = dikhāʼo+Prk. अट्टं = S. क+त्वं
def: Sight, view; exhibition, representation; show, display, ostentation, pomp, pageantry; speciousness, pretence, disguise.

ety: dikhāʼo and dikhāwā, i.q. dikhāʼū, q.v.; — dikhāwaṭ = dikhāʼo+Prk. अट्टं = S. क+त्वं
def: Sight, view; exhibition, representation; show, display, ostentation, pomp, pageantry; speciousness, pretence, disguise.

ety: dikhāʼo and dikhāwā, i.q. dikhāʼū, q.v.; — dikhāwaṭ = dikhāʼo+Prk. अट्टं = S. क+त्वं
def: Sight, view; exhibition, representation; show, display, ostentation, pomp, pageantry; speciousness, pretence, disguise.
adj. For show, pretended, specious, false, forged.
v.t. (dialec.) = dikhānā, q.v.
s.m. Name of a certain tribe of Rājpūts.
s.m. = dīćhit, q.v.
s.f. = dikhāʼī, q.v.
v.t. To show; to direct; to indicate, &c. ( = dikhānā, q.v.); to present the appearance of, to make a show of, to pretend.
v.t. (dialec.) = dikh-lānā, q.v.
s.m. 1˚ = dikhāwā, q.v.; — 2˚ One who shows the way, a guide, conductor.
v.n. = dikhnā, and disnā, q.v.
v.n. To be seen; to appear; to seem; — v.t. (dialec.) = dekhnā, q.v.
s.f. = dikhāwaṭ, q.v.
s.m. One who looks on, beholder, spectator, viewer, observer.

adj. & s.m. Thin, slender; minute; subtle, &c. ( = daqīq, q.v.); hectic (e.g. tap-ě-diq, 'a hectic fever'); indisposed, ailing; troubled, disturbed; vexed, annoyed, irritated, teased, worried, harassed, plagued; — a hectic fever ( = tap-ě-diq); difficulty, trouble, worry, &c.
subs: adj. Troublesome, teasing; in trouble, or affliction, or difficulty; distressed, pinched
s.f. Trouble, vexation, worry; distress, poverty, penury; — diq-dārī uṭhānā, v.n. To undergo trouble, &c.; to take trouble, to be at the pains (to, -kī)
v.t. To tease, irritate, annoy, trouble, harass, worry, plague
v.t. To worry or plague to death, to trouble or annoy exceedingly, to pester, torment, &c.
v.n. To be indisposed, be ailing (e.g. t̤abīʻat diq hai); to be irritated, be annoyed, or worried, &c.

ety: for A. دقة, inf. n. of دقّ 'to be slender,' &c.; with amplification of signification
def: s.f. A minute or subtle thing, knotty or nice point; delicate matter; difficulty; abstruseness, intricacy; trouble, perplexity, difficulty, distress; — diligence, close application, industry; accuracy
subs: adj. Understanding subtleties, &c.; penetrating; discerning; quick of apprehension
To fall into difficulties, get into trouble, be in distress.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دِقَّت (diqqat, “accuracy; attention”), itself from Arabic دِقَّة (diqqa).

def: s.f. Attack, onset; butting with the horns (as an animal).
s.m. Initiation as the pupil or disciple of some saint or religious guide (vulg. daććhā).

s.f. Preparation or consecration (for a religious ceremony); a ceremony preliminary to a sacrifice, initiation, dedication; receiving the initiatory mantra or prayer; undertaking religious observances, or engaging in a course of austerities (for a special purpose), devotion; sacrificing, offering oblations, dedicating oneself (to an object, or to a person), self-devotion.

part. adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Consecrated, initiated; prepared, ready (for a sacrifice, &c.); performed (as the dīkshā ceremony); — one who has received initiation, or by whom the preparatory ceremonies have been observed; a disciple or pupil (of a Brāhman).
s.m. Consecrator, spiritual father, one who initiates, the communicator of the initiatory mantra or prayer.
s.m. Consecrator, spiritual father, one who initiates, the communicator of the initiatory mantra or prayer.
s.m. Heart; mind, soul; spirit, valour (see jī)
subs: dil-ućaṭnā (-se), The heart to turn (from), to be weary (of), be disgusted (with); to be alienated (from)
adj. & s.m.f. Heart-tormenting, vexing, cruel; — a sweetheart
s.f. Torment of the heart, vexation of spirit, anxiety, trouble
adj. Troubled in mind, dejected; vexed, displeased, offended
s.m. 'Mind-soothing'; soothing, comfort, consolation, encouragement; — dilāsā denā (-ko), To soothe, console, comfort; to encourage
adj. Heart-broken, dejected, melancholy, pensive ( = dil-figār)
v.n. The heart to be greatly perturbed, to be uneasy to distraction
(-par), To fall, or be, in love (with)
adj. Suspending the soul (with delight), heart-ravishing, transporting, delightful, charming, engaging
s.f. Fluttering or palpitation of the heart; rashness
s.m. Interchange of hearts, mutual love
adj. & s.m. Heart-ravishing, captivating, attractive, charming, lovely, beloved; — heart-ravisher, a lovely person, a sweetheart
(apnā), To take offence, become displeased; — to be sick, to vomit
adj. Having the heart turned (from) or set (against, -se), averse (to), indifferent (to), dissatisfied, disgusted (with)
s.f. Captivation of the heart; winning of the hearts or affections (of people); attraction, charm, loveliness; pacification, comfort
(-kā), To give (one) heart, to inspirit, encourage
adj. 'Heart-bound'; afflicted; attached, enamoured, in love
s.f. Affliction or anguish of mind; attachment, friendship, love
adj. & s.m. Attractive, charming, lovely; — a beloved child, a son
v.n. To be amused, or diverted, &c.
(-kā), To find out the mind or disposition (of), to discover what is agreeable or pleasing (to anyone); to find one agreeable
adj. Acceptable to the mind or soul, pleasant, delightful, amiable, approved (syn. dil-pasand)
(kisī ke), To act according to the wishes (of), to be obedient (to)
adj. Grateful to the heart, satisfactory or pleasing to the mind, pleasing, desirable, agreeable; esteemed; plausible, &c. (syn. dil-paẕīr)
(-kā), One's intentions to be discovered, or detected
v.n. The heart to be breaking; to be in great sorrow or distress of mind
(-se), The heart to turn away (from); to be sick or weary (of), be disgusted (with)
v.n. The heart to be captivated, to fall in love
(kā), To turn (one) away (from), to render (one) disinclined
v.n. The heart to be turned back (from); to forget one's sorrow, to be consoled, be quieted, be composed
s.f. Heart-burning, distress of mind; love
adj. Distressed, mournful, sad; — miserly, niggard ( = tang-dil)
(kā), To break the heart (of), to dishearten, discourage; to disappoint, to mortify
dil ṭhaharnā, v.n. The heart to be at rest, to be quieted, be comforted or consoled
v.n. To be assured, be satisfied
v.n. The heart to burn, to be moved or affected
adj. Collected in mind, assured, contented, cheerful ( = ḵẖāt̤ir-jamʻ, q.v.)
(apnā), To keep the mind at ease, to rest assured
s.f. Ease of mind, assurance, confidence; security, content
(-kī), To set (one's) mind at rest or ease, to give assurance or confidence (to), to satisfy
adj. Heart-seeking, studious of pleasing; grateful, agreeable, desirable
s.f. Seeking to gain the heart (of another); study to please; attention; sympathy
(kī), To study the inclination or wish (of), to try to please, &c.
(-kā), To steal the heart (of); to draw the heart away (from, -se), to abstain (from), to be deterred and desist (from an enterprise); to be inattentive (to), to neglect, &c., see jī ćhipānā
adj. Beloved, pleasant, delightful, interesting, alluring, engaging, charming
adj. (f. -ī), Persevering; brave; resolute; enterprising; generous
(-meṅ), To stimulate the heart (in); to be brave (in)
(-par), The heart to be set (on), to desire
adj. Heart-ravishing, captivating; — inattentive, negligent; — timid
v.n. To have presence of mind
adj. Heart-rending, horrible, excruciating, vexatious; grieved to the heart
s.f. Wounding of the heart, torment
adj. & s.m. Beloved; desirable; desiring; in love; — what one likes or longs for, heart's desire, a beloved object; desire, affection
(-kā), To gladden or cheer the heart (of), to please, amuse, divert
adj. Who has given his heart
adj. & s.m.f. Possessing or winning the heart, delighting the heart, charming; — having heart, encouraged; — a lover, mistress, sweetheart (syn. dil-bar)
s.f. Demonstrations of love, kindness, blandishment; consolation; encouragement
s.f. Giving the heart (to a thing or work), heartiness, earnestness, alacrity; — consolation; encouragement
(-kā), To look into the heart (of); to study the temper (of), ascertain the inclinations or wishes (of)
(-ko), To give the mind (to), to apply oneself diligently (to); to lose the heart (to), to be in love
adj. & s.m.f. Heart-ravishing, bewitching, alluring; — a charmer, sweetheart
(-kā), To possess the heart or affection (of); to show consideration for the feelings or wishes (of), to please, gratify, oblige; to comfort, console; — to encourage; to conciliate
adj. Wounded to the heart, afflicted; in love
s.f. Wound of the heart; affliction
adj. Wounded or stricken to the heart
s.f. Pleasing the heart, delighting; ardour
(apnā), To take heart or courage, to nerve (oneself to an effort)

adj. & s.m.f. Heart-burning, heart-inflaming; beloved; moving, affecting, touching, pathetic; sympathetic; compassionate, benevolent; passionate, ardent, fervent; — a friend; a sweetheart; — dil-soz ḵẖāna-tarāsh, s.m. An enemy under the semblance of a friend
s.f. Heart-burning, warmth of feeling; ardour, fervour; affection; sympathy, compassion
adj. Whose heart is lost, enamoured, in love; bereft of reason, deprived of the senses
adj. Heart-enlightening; heart-cheering, delightful, pleasant, recreating, refreshing
adj. Heart-alluring, enticing, bewitching, enchanting, fascinating, charming, beautiful, lovely
adj. Heart-broken, mournful, dejected, sad, melancholy, pensive; — an epithet of a sweetheart
or dil-kaṛwā karnā, 'To harden the heart,' to summon up courage or resolution (for)
adj. Heart-attracting, alluring, attractive, winning, engaging, pleasant; beloved, approved, chosen
adj. Heart-expanding, blissful, delightful, charming, exhilarating
s.f. Attractiveness, winningness, female attraction, loveliness
(-ke), To touch the heart, to affect; to approve itself (to)
(-kā), To turn the heart (of anyone) against (one), to displease, offend

or toṛnā (apne, or kisī-ke), To relieve the mind (of pain; or of a feeling of ill-will, or revenge, &c.); to take revenge, pay off old scores; to minister consolation (to); — to rip up old sores, to renew a half-forgotten grief
v.n. The mind to be relieved, &c.
adj. Warm-hearted, animated, ardent, fervent; full of desire, brave
s.f. Warm-heartedness, warmth of heart; friendship; ardour, alacrity
adj. Seizing or captivating the heart; terrifying, filling with horror or anguish; heart-stricken, oppressed in mind, afflicted, concerned, grieved (at or with, -se), low-spirited, melancholy, sad
s.f. Affliction, sorrow, concern, sadness, melancholy, &c.
To set the heart or mind (on, -meṅ), give or apply the mind (to); to intend; to give the heart (to), fall in love (with, -se)
v.n. The heart to be given or applied (to, -meṅ), to be attentive (to); to be attached (to, -par, or -kī t̤araf), to be enamoured (of), be in love (with)
s.f. Application of mind, attention, diligence; inclination; attachment, friendship; diversion, merriment, joking, fun, jest
(-kā), To captivate or win the heart (of, to discover or ascertain the wishes or feelings (of)
adv. As the heart might wish, to the heart's desire
To press the heart and be still, to bear quietly or patiently
To keep or bear a stout heart
To cloud the heart (with grief, or vexation, &c.), to take to heart, to be grieved or displeased
(-ko), To place in the heart, to cherish, love
(-ke), To put one's own heart or mind into (another), to cause one to think or feel as oneself does; to exercise influence (over); to possess the heart (of)
(-ke), To put into the heart (of), to inspire
(-ko), To keep hidden in the mind, keep to oneself; to bear in mind, to remember
(-ke), To feel distrustful (of), to distrust, suspect
(-ke), To obtain a place in the heart (of), to possess the heart (of)
(-ke), 'To take up (one's, or its) abode in the heart (of), to win the heart or affections (of), to contract intimate friendship (with)
adj. Heart-residing, impressed on, or implanted in, the mind; forming a subject of constant thought; grateful to the mind, agreeable, pleasing
(-ke), To implant in the mind (of), to fix or impress on the mind (of)
adj. & s.m.f. Soothing the mind, soothing; gracious, conciliating; beloved; — a mistress, sweetheart
s.m. Anything on which the heart is fixed, an object of affection, a sweetheart; attention
adj. (f. -ī), Spirited, courageous, bold; large-hearted, generous, liberal
s.m. Heart and soul; tooth and nail; — dil-o-jān-se, adv. With heart and soul, heartily, cheerfully
s.m. 'Heart and brain'; loftiness of soul; ambition; pride; stateliness
adv. To the very heart, to the heart's core
To lose heart, to be faint-hearted; to lose command over oneself
(-se, or -kī t̤araf-se), The heart to turn (against), to be averse (to)
(-par), The heart to be set (on); to be tender (towards)
adv. In the inmost heart; to oneself, silently
s.m. A secret; — adv. Kept secret; secretly, to oneself ( = dil-hī dil-meṅ)
adv. With one mind, with one consent, unanimously
To show largeness of heart, to practise liberality, be very liberal: be-dil, adj. Heartless; dispirited, discouraged; unwilling, averse; — s.m. One who has lost his heart, a lover
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دِل (dil). First attested in c. 1265 as Old Hindi دل (dl) and later as Middle Hindi دل (dl).

def: s.f. (local), A small eminence; site of an old village (cf. dillī).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian دِل (dil). First attested in c. 1265 as Old Hindi دل (dl) and later as Middle Hindi دل (dl).

ety: prob. another form of ḍaul, q.v.; and cf. ḍher, ḍhelā, and ḍel
def: s.m. Bulk, size, stature, body; figure, form, shape, likeness
subs: s.m. Size and frame, figure, shape.

def: voc. O heart!
s.f. = dilhā, q.v.
v.t. To cause to give, cause to give up, cause to yield, cause to pay; to put in possession; to occasion; to consign; to assign
subs: v.t. To obtain or get back, to recover
s.m. One who causes (another) to give or give up, &c.

def: s.m. See s.v. dil.
adj. See s.v. dil.

r: diler, dil, er, yār, wār, war, āwar
ety: dil+er = yār = wār or war = āwar, q.v.
def: adj. Intrepid, bold, daring, venturesome, brave, courageous, valiant; animated; insolent, presumptuous, impudent.

ety: diler+āna = Zend āna or ana = S. ana
def: adv. Boldly, intrepidly, &c.
s.f. Intrepidity, boldness, daring, courage, valour, bravery, venturesomeness, hardihood, hardiness; assurance, insolence, presumption
subs: (-meṅ), To be bold, or venturesome, &c. (in); to make bold (to).

def: s.f. A panel.

ety: dil, q.v.+ī = Zend aêna = S. इन्
def: adj. Of the heart, hearty, cordial; sincere, true; intimate.
s.m. Name of a king of Dilī or Dillī, and ancestor of Rāma.

ety: also callcd Shāhjahānābād; — the ancient Hastināpur; cf. S. dilī-pa
def: s.m. A native, or an inhabitant, of Dillī.

r: dilwaiyā, dilwā, nā, aiyā, waiyā, dal-waiyā
def: s.m. One who causes to be given, or given up, or to be paid.
